faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Secaucus homes?
faqs.answer: Secaucus residents most commonly encounter Indian meal moths, sawtoothed grain beetles, flour beetles, and rice weevils. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, pasta, nuts, and pet food. They can enter homes through contaminated groceries or find their way in from nearby warehouses and storage facilities common in the Secaucus area.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: After treatment, store all dry goods in airtight containers, regularly clean pantry shelves and corners, inspect groceries before storing them, and rotate older items to the front. We'll provide specific recommendations based on your Secaucus property's layout and vulnerabilities. Our technicians will also seal potential entry points and advise on proper food storage practices to maintain a pest-free pantry.
